overview: Andhra University College of Engineering for Women (AUCEW), established in 2010 in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh, is a public, self-financed constituent engineering college of Andhra University. Built exclusively for women to foster technical leadership and academic excellence, the college offers dedicated undergraduate B.Tech and integrated dual-degree programs in fundamental and computing engineering streams.
accreditation: AICTE Approved, Affiliated directly to Andhra University, and NAAC Accredited with 'A' Grade (scoring 3.6 on a 4-point scale).

admissions.admissionProcess: Admissions are governed strictly through state merit ranks achieved via the AP EAPCET entrance examination, followed by the standard centralized counseling process administered by APSCHE.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET usually in May; Counseling registrations usually start around July-August. Refer to the official website for real-time updates.

campus.area: Located within the main 400+ acre campus of Andhra University
campus.specialNote: The campus shares major technical infrastructure and strategic support resources with the parent Andhra University College of Engineering (Autonomous) to maximize laboratory depth and corporate connection.

contact.address: A.U. College of Engineering for Women, Maddilapalem, Visakhapatnam - 530013, Andhra Pradesh, India
